records.ety: [NL., fr. Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/ (sc. <?/<?/<?/) a dried body, a mummy, fr. <?/<?/<?/<?/ dried up, parched, <?/<?/<?/<?/ to dry, dry up, parch.]

defs: The bony and cartilaginous framework which supports the soft parts of a vertebrate animal.
The more or less firm or hardened framework of an invertebrate animal.
notes: [See Illust. of the Human Skeleton, in Appendix.]
☞ In a wider sense, the skeleton includes the whole connective-tissue framework with the integument and its appendages. See Endoskeleton, and Exoskeleton.

defs: A very thin or lean person.
The framework of anything; the principal parts that support the rest, but without the appendages.
The heads and outline of a literary production, especially of a sermon.
quotes: The great skeleton of the world.

defs: Consisting of, or resembling, a skeleton; consisting merely of the framework or outlines; having only certain leading features of anything; as, a skeleton sermon; a skeleton crystal.
